channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo XIV Delivers Historic Address to Spanish Parliament, Demands Respect for Migrants
item.description: In an unprecedented speech to Spain's Congress of Deputies, Pope Leo XIV called for respecting migrants and received a 7-minute standing ovation, marking the first time a pope has addressed the Spanish parliament. (AP News)

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Announces Fresh Sanctions Package Banning Russian Soldiers From Entering Bloc
item.description: The European Union unveiled its 21st sanctions package targeting Moscow, including a ban on Russian soldiers entering the bloc and new measures against individuals and entities supporting Russia's war effort. (The Guardian)

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK and Allies Impose Sanctions on Firms Enabling West Bank Settler Violence
item.description: Britain joined western allies in sanctioning networks enabling extremist settler violence against Palestinians in the West Bank, with France also barring far-right Israeli Minister Bezalel Smotrich from entry. (BBC)

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Vows US Response After Saying Iran Shot Down US Army Helicopter Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump stated that Iran shot down a US Apache helicopter and that the US "must respond," though he confirmed the two crew members were rescued safely by an American sea drone. (CNN)

item.title: 🇨🇳 Pentagon Labels Alibaba and BYD as Companies Aiding Chinese Military
item.description: The US Defense Department designated major Chinese tech firms including Alibaba and BYD as military-linked companies, escalating tensions between Washington and Beijing over technology and security concerns. (NPR)

item.title: 🚜⛽ US Farmers Hit by Record Diesel Prices as Iran War Chokes Fuel Supplies
item.description: High energy costs are squeezing grain and soybean growers across the US farm belt as the Iran war chokes fuel supplies through the Strait of Hormuz, pushing diesel prices to record highs in key agricultural states. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇱🇧💥 Israel Strikes Tyre, Killing 8, Then Orders Evacuation of Entire Lebanese City
item.description: Israel struck the historic port city of Tyre in southern Lebanon killing at least 8 people, minutes later ordering the evacuation of the entire city for the first time in the conflict. (Reuters)

item.title: 🏛️🇺🇸 US House Advances $70 Billion Bill to Fund Trump Immigration Crackdown
item.description: House Republicans have advanced a $70 billion immigration enforcement bill with a final vote expected later Tuesday, funding Trump immigration actions for the remainder of his term. (AP News)

item.title: ☀️ Hanwha Qcells launches first fully onshore US solar supply chain
item.description: South Korean company begins solar cell production in the US, establishing the country’s first integrated supply chain amid push for clean energy independence. (Nikkei Asia)

item.title: 🇵🇸 UN report documents systematic rights violations against Palestinians
item.description: UN inquiry finds civilians in Gaza and West Bank subjected to severe rights violations by Israeli forces and settlers, as tensions escalate in the region. (SCMP)

item.title: 🇩🇰 Denmark fails to disclose soaring F-35 costs, auditors say
item.description: Danish State Auditors criticize Defense Ministry for not properly informing lawmakers about the true price of one of the country's largest defense purchases. (Anadolu Agency)

item.title: ⚡ Japanese-Canadian consortium to buy European wind and solar farms for AI data centers
item.description: Mitsubishi HC Capital partners with Brookfield to acquire renewable energy plants in Europe to power growing AI infrastructure demands. (Nikkei Asia)

item.title: 🚁 Trump says Iran downed US Apache helicopter near Strait of Hormuz, vows response
item.description: President Trump announced Iran shot down a US Army Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and declared the US "must, of necessity, respond." The two pilots were rescued unharmed by a Navy sea drone in what marks the first such rescue operation for the US military. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU proposes 21st sanctions package targeting Russian banks and crypto networks
item.description: The European Union proposed its 21st round of sanctions against Russia, heavily targeting banks, crypto networks, drone production, and oil traders. EU chief diplomat Kaja Kallas announced the sweeping measures aimed at increasing pressure on Moscow over the Ukraine war. (Reuters)

item.title: ⬇️ Trump approval near record low as Americans brace for higher gas prices
item.description: A Reuters poll shows only 22% of Americans approve of Trump's handling of the cost of living, with most expecting gasoline prices to rise further amid the Iran conflict. The finding underscores political pressure on the administration as the Middle East crisis drags on. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇳🇬 Cholera outbreak in Nigeria's Borno state kills 74, infects over 7,000
item.description: A cholera outbreak in northeastern Nigeria's conflict-ridden Borno state has killed 74 people and infected more than 7,000 others since May. The outbreak adds to the humanitarian crisis in a region already devastated by years of insurgency. (AP News)

item.title: 🐟 Russia faces cod export ban under proposed EU sanctions
item.description: Russian cod could be banned from EU markets in the latest sanctions package aimed at pressuring Moscow to end its invasion of Ukraine. The measure would target one of Russia's key export industries and further strain EU-Russia trade relations. (The Telegraph)

item.title: 🇪🇺 Strongmen wrong to loathe Europe, The Economist argues
item.description: An analysis challenges the contempt of Trump, Xi, and Putin for European soft power, arguing Europe's economic and diplomatic leverage remains formidable despite their dismissive rhetoric. (The Economist)

item.title: 🚀 NASA names Artemis III crew for next step toward Moon landing
item.description: The space agency announced the four astronauts who will crew the Artemis III mission in 2027, marking the next major milestone in America's return to lunar exploration. (BBC)

item.title: ⚔️ Middle East crisis live: US helicopter downed as fragile Iran-Israel truce holds
item.description: Comprehensive live coverage of escalating tensions after Iran shot down a US Apache helicopter, with evacuation orders in Lebanon and diplomatic efforts to prevent wider conflict. (The Guardian)

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel Uses Sound of Crying Children to Lure Civilians in South Lebanon, Report Claims
item.description: Israeli drones in southern Lebanon are allegedly using recordings of crying children to lure civilians into the open, according to a Middle East Eye investigation. The tactic raises serious concerns about violations of international humanitarian law. (Middle East Eye)

item.title: 🇨🇳 Microsoft Cuts Hundreds of Cloud Jobs in China as Data Laws Tighten
item.description: Microsoft is laying off hundreds of employees in its China cloud operations as both Beijing and Washington intensify data security regulations, signaling growing tech decoupling between the two powers. (SCMP)

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU to Invest €5 Billion in North Africa, Middle East Renewables
item.description: The European Union plans major investments in renewable energy infrastructure across North Africa and the Middle East, aiming to harness African sunlight to power Europes green transition while reducing energy dependence on Russia. (Politico)

item.title: 🇨🇳 Xi Jinping Visits North Korean War Monument, Emphasizes Eternal Ties
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping visited a North Korean war monument, evoking what state media called eternal historical memory between the two nations, in a symbolic gesture amid regional tensions. (SCMP)

item.title: 🇺🇸 Fed to Hold Rates Through 2026 as War-Driven Inflation Persists
item.description: The US Federal Reserve will keep interest rates unchanged for the rest of 2026, according to a Reuters poll of economists. War-induced inflation has proven more persistent than expected, delaying rate cuts. (Reuters)
